candidate_scenario: An attacker pretends to be a supplier.
rationale: Rewritten around IP package identity and intake evidence.

candidate_scenario: A file in the project directory is changed.
rationale: Rewritten by artifact family because not every file has the same hardware consequence.

candidate_scenario: A user denies running a build.
rationale: Rewritten around source-to-bitstream lineage and workflow identity.

candidate_scenario: A build server exposes files.
rationale: Rewritten around source, report, and bitstream confidentiality.

candidate_scenario: The build machine becomes unavailable.
rationale: Retained only when artifact manipulation affects timing closure or routing availability.

candidate_scenario: A user gains administrator privileges.
rationale: Rewritten around signing, release, and programming authority.

candidate_scenario: A configuration file is modified.
rationale: Split into constraints versus tool options because evidence differs.

candidate_scenario: Reports may leak sensitive data.
rationale: Rewritten around timing paths, resource use, IP composition, and constraints.

candidate_scenario: A wrong hardware image is deployed.
rationale: Rewritten as bitstream substitution or mislabeling.

candidate_scenario: Intermediate output is modified.
rationale: Rewritten around generated netlist/checkpoint lineage.

candidate_scenario: Implementation state is reused.
rationale: Rewritten around stale checkpoint or route-state reuse.

candidate_scenario: A report cannot be verified.
rationale: Rewritten around report-to-source/configuration/tool linkage.

candidate_scenario: A release package is missing metadata.
rationale: Rewritten as missing FPGA artifact digest set and attestation.

candidate_scenario: A CI token can publish artifacts.
rationale: Rewritten around FPGA release/signing authority.

candidate_scenario: A binary artifact is replaced.
rationale: Rewritten because a bitstream is a hardware configuration, not an arbitrary binary.

candidate_scenario: Secret material leaks from a release bundle.
rationale: Rewritten around bitstream signing/encryption material.

candidate_scenario: A clock constraint is relaxed.
rationale: Merged into constraint tampering.

candidate_scenario: A false path is inserted.
rationale: Merged into constraint tampering.

candidate_scenario: A pin location is changed.
rationale: Merged into constraint tampering.

candidate_scenario: A synthesis seed or option is changed.
rationale: Merged into tool option/script tampering.

candidate_scenario: A route seed is changed.
rationale: Merged into implementation-state manipulation.

candidate_scenario: A timing report is edited.
rationale: Merged into signoff report tampering.

candidate_scenario: A DRC report is suppressed.
rationale: Merged into signoff report tampering.

candidate_scenario: A stale bitstream is relabeled.
rationale: Merged into bitstream substitution or mislabeling.

candidate_scenario: An operator programs the wrong or spoofed target.

candidate_scenario: A release log is deleted.
rationale: Merged into missing durable release provenance.

rationale: No FPGA artifact is named.

candidate_scenario: Traffic between developer machines is sniffed.
rationale: No FPGA artifact or release boundary is specified.

candidate_scenario: Corporate network outage delays engineering work.
rationale: Generic availability risk without artifact-specific consequence.

candidate_scenario: Malware modifies arbitrary files.
rationale: Too broad until rewritten around source, constraints, reports, or bitstreams.

candidate_scenario: An employee denies changing a ticket.
rationale: No FPGA toolchain artifact or authority boundary.

candidate_scenario: A user gains local administrator access.
rationale: Generic unless linked to signing, release, or programming authority.

candidate_scenario: A cloud bucket exposes unrelated documents.
rationale: No modeled FPGA artifact.

candidate_scenario: A failed OS patch breaks a workstation.
rationale: Generic IT operation risk without FPGA artifact mapping.

candidate_scenario: Side-channel measurement extracts a secret from a configured FPGA.
rationale: Out of development-toolchain scope.

candidate_scenario: Voltage fault injection changes device behavior.
rationale: Physical/chip-level attack, not toolchain artifact modeling.

candidate_scenario: An attacker probes board traces after deployment.
rationale: Physical probing is outside scope.

candidate_scenario: Laser fault injection flips configuration memory.
rationale: Chip-level fault model, not development artifact lineage.

candidate_scenario: Power analysis recovers a bitstream encryption key.
rationale: Cryptanalytic/side-channel threat outside the modeled flow.

candidate_scenario: An attacker overheats a deployed FPGA.
rationale: Runtime physical availability threat.

candidate_scenario: A counterfeit board is installed in the field.
rationale: Hardware supply-chain/device identity scope, not development-toolchain artifact scope.

candidate_scenario: A proprietary optimizer silently introduces malicious behavior with no observable artifact change.
rationale: No evidence requirement under the paper's vendor-neutral artifact model.

candidate_scenario: A malicious action occurs somewhere in the toolchain but no artifact, actor, or boundary is identified.
rationale: Underspecified candidate with no reviewable record.
